creator: gary@hotlava.com
text:
I think this could be due to not doing a 'make clean; make depend' when upgrading kernels.

I  recently had precisely this error, and found that I had not done the clean/depend when upgrading.

Try it and let me know.

If this is the fault, then bug report should be that make clean and depend should be the default, with only experts overrriding it.  Otherwise we'll (the BSD boys) get a bad reputation for reliaiblity.

Gary
